skill.name: firecrawl-build-search
skill.description: Guides developers who need to integrate Firecrawl search into product code, agent workflows, and source discovery pipelines.
skill.summary: Build query-first Firecrawl search workflows before deeper scraping or extraction.
skill.author: firecrawl

security_audit.summary: The skill is a documentation-only guide, and no executable code, command execution, prompt injection attempt, or credential exfiltration logic was found. Static command, weak-crypto, and path-traversal findings are false positives from markdown code spans and relative links. The real risk is legitimate use of a Firecrawl API key and external Firecrawl network requests, so publication should include a clear credential and network warning.

medium_findings.title: External Firecrawl API Key and Network Dependency
medium_findings.description: The skill declares FIRECRAWL_API_KEY as a required input and points users to hosted or self-hosted Firecrawl endpoints. This is expected for the skill purpose, but users must treat the key as sensitive and understand that searches contact external services.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The API key requirement and documentation links are explicit. The risk is legitimate service use rather than malicious code because the file contains guidance only.

low_findings.title: Markdown Code Spans Misidentified as Command Execution
low_findings.description: Static findings for Ruby or shell backtick execution are false positives. The flagged text is markdown inline code for Firecrawl routes and product terms, not executable shell or Ruby code.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The entire file is markdown and the flagged backticks surround route names such as /search. There is no script block or executable command context.

low_findings.title: Relative Markdown Links Misidentified as Path Traversal
low_findings.description: The ../ sequences are relative links to related skill documentation. They do not perform filesystem reads, writes, or path-based access control decisions.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The path traversal pattern appears only inside markdown links. No code consumes these paths or accesses the filesystem.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography and Dangerous Combination Heuristics Not Confirmed
low_findings.description: The static weak-cryptography and combined code-execution heuristics are not supported by the file contents. No cryptographic operation, obfuscated payload, executable code, prompt injection text, or data exfiltration behavior was found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review found documentation metadata, API input descriptions, and links only. The heuristic combination lacks executable code or credential transmission logic in the skill file.

content.value_statement: Many apps need to discover useful URLs before scraping or summarizing content. This skill helps Claude, Codex, and Claude Code plan Firecrawl search integrations with clear query and extraction boundaries.
actual_capabilities: Identifies when a query-first search flow is more suitable than starting from a known URL.
Guides separation of search, ranking, scraping, and interaction responsibilities.
Points developers to Firecrawl language documentation for Node, Python, Rust, Java, Elixir, and cURL.
Recommends selective follow-up extraction to manage latency and cost.
Frames search results as source discovery inputs for later product workflows.
limitations: Does not include executable integration code or SDK examples.
Requires a Firecrawl API key for hosted Firecrawl requests.
Does not validate search result quality, ranking accuracy, or source trust.
Does not replace the official Firecrawl API documentation.

use_cases.title: Add Source Discovery to an AI Product
use_cases.description: Plan a search step that finds candidate sources before answer generation or summarization.

use_cases.title: Design Research Agent Workflows
use_cases.description: Define how an agent should search the web, shortlist pages, and decide when deeper extraction is needed.

use_cases.title: Prototype Query-to-URL Pipelines
use_cases.description: Convert user queries into ranked URLs that can later be scraped or processed by another workflow.

prompt_templates.title: Choose the Right Firecrawl Flow
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me decide whether my feature should use Firecrawl search, scrape, or interact. My user starts with this input: [describe input].

prompt_templates.title: Plan a Search Integration
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a Firecrawl search workflow for [product feature]. Include query input, result fields, follow-up extraction rules, and failure handling.

prompt_templates.title: Refine Search and Extraction Boundaries
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this search workflow and separate discovery, ranking, scraping, and interaction responsibilities: [paste workflow].

prompt_templates.title: Optimize an Agent Search Pipeline
prompt_templates.prompt: Improve this Firecrawl agent search pipeline for cost, latency, source quality, and predictable downstream scraping: [paste design].

output_examples.input: A product team wants an assistant to answer current market questions with sources.
output_examples.output: The skill recommends a query-first search step, a short ranked source list, and selective scraping only for promising results.

output_examples.input: A developer already has a known URL and wants page content.
output_examples.output: The skill directs the developer away from search and toward the related scrape workflow.

output_examples.input: An agent needs to find companies, then inspect selected websites.
output_examples.output: The skill frames search as discovery and suggests follow-up extraction only after source selection.

best_practices: Use search only when the product starts from a query instead of a known URL.
Keep query contracts stable so downstream scraping logic remains predictable.
Limit hydration and follow-up extraction to results that matter.
anti_patterns: Do not use search when the target URL is already known.
Do not combine search and full extraction unless the product needs both.
Do not send secrets, private user data, or unnecessary context in search queries.

faq.question: Does this skill run Firecrawl searches by itself?
faq.answer: No. It is guidance for designing and implementing Firecrawl search workflows.

faq.question: Does it require a Firecrawl API key?
faq.answer: Yes. Hosted Firecrawl requests require the FIRECRAWL_API_KEY input.

faq.question: When should I use search instead of scrape?
faq.answer: Use search when the user starts with a topic or question and you need to discover URLs first.

faq.question: Can it support self-hosted Firecrawl?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill documents an optional FIRECRAWL_API_URL input for self-hosted deployments.

faq.question: Does it include language-specific code?
faq.answer: No. It links to Firecrawl source-of-truth documentation for several languages.

faq.question: Is this skill safe to publish?
faq.answer: Yes, with a medium-risk warning because it uses network services and an API key.
